app.gitMmLaunchTitle: Open Git MM multi-repo window (Beta; requires git mm on PATH)

gitMm.lead: Typical flow: git mm init, then git mm sync, then click Start and enter the branch name to run git mm start. Child repos come from scanning nested .git. Requires git mm on PATH.
gitMm.uploadPromptFallback: The script asks for [Y/n]. Choose Y or N.
gitMm.uploadHint: Runs git mm upload; [Y/n] prompts open a dialog and send y or n to the process.
gitMm.syncParallelHint: Passed to git mm sync -j. Higher values increase concurrency (CPU, disk & network limits apply).
gitMm.mmBlockingWork: git mm is running — sub-repo area locked to avoid conflicting operations
gitMm.mmFinishedNonZero: git mm finished (exit {code}). ERROR lines in the log are often from your manifest/scripts, not this app—check the full output and repos.
gitMm.mmErrorLinesHint: ERROR lines are usually from git mm or your scripts, not a failure in this client—verify with repo state and the log below.
gitMm.mmTimeoutHint: Each run is capped at about 15 minutes; the process is then killed. A spinning button usually means the child is waiting for input (credentials) or is still working.
gitMm.mmRunningHint: When it finishes, exit code and duration appear below; we still show a panel if there is no output.
gitMm.mmNoOutput: No stdout/stderr. If repos did not update, run the same command in a terminal from this folder.
gitMm.startBranchPh: For git mm start, e.g. feature/foo
gitMm.startDialogHint: After sync, enter the branch to check out; runs git mm start <branch>. Your last input is remembered.
gitMm.noSubrepos: No nested Git repos found; try sync or another folder
gitMm.emptyWorkspaces: Add a workspace root (typically your git mm workspace directory)
gitMm.pickFile: Pick a file to view diff
gitMm.pickCommit: Select a commit in the list
gitMm.pickFileFromCommit: Click a file for its diff in this commit
gitMm.changesInPanelHint: Stage, commit, and line-level diff here—same Git operations as in that repo. Use the link when you need the main Changes tree.
gitMm.openInMainForAdvancedFeatures: Switched to the main window with this repo. Use Blame, file history, and interactive staging (git add -p) there.

ribbon.comparePrTitle: Open hosting compare page (GitHub / GitLab / Bitbucket)

changes.conflictHint: Resolve with ours/theirs or an external tool. Side-by-side view does not show conflict markers like {marker}; switch to single-column diff to jump between markers.
changes.conflictJumpTooltip: Side-by-side HTML diff hides conflict markers. Switch to single-column text diff to see markers and jump.
changes.pickFile: Select a file on the left to view changes
changes.invalidDiffSelection: Selection does not overlap any diff hunk
changes.binaryNoSize: Could not read file size (may be deleted)
changes.discardSecondConfirmMsg: Please confirm again: this action cannot be undone.
changes.discardConfirmMsg: Restore selected files’ unstaged changes from the index? This cannot be undone.
changes.discardStagedSelectionConfirmMsg: Discard the staged changes in the selection and restore the working tree to match HEAD for those lines? This cannot be undone.
changes.diffSelectionExpanded: Selection was expanded to full diff hunks before applying
changes.diffSelectionContextAdded: Added a few context lines inside each hunk so the patch applies cleanly
changes.noPathsChecked: Select files first (click or Ctrl/Shift multi-select), or right-click a file row.
changes.sideBySideNoLineOps: Line staging needs single-column diff. Switch view in Diff options.
changes.binaryNoLineOps: Binary files do not support line operations
changes.wrongScopeLineOps: This action does not match the current diff scope.
changes.conflictNoLineOps: Conflicted files do not support line staging/discard until resolved.
changes.lineDiscardStagedOk: Discarded staged selection (index and working tree updated)
changes.discardUntrackedHint: All selected paths are untracked; `git restore` cannot discard them. Delete manually or use git clean.
changes.discardSkippedUntracked: Untracked paths were skipped; only tracked files were restored from the index.

settings.title: Settings
settings.appearanceLead: Theme applies to Element Plus components and the main shell.
settings.diffLead: Default diff options when a repository opens; you can still change them in Changes.
settings.toolsLead: Choose the shell used for “open in Git terminal” and the external merge tool for conflicts.
settings.customGitShellPathPlaceholder: Empty = built-in order (e.g. Git Bash, Windows Terminal)
settings.customGitShellPathHint: Full path to a terminal (e.g. wt.exe, PowerShell, cmd). Used for ribbon “Open → Open in Git terminal” and submodule “Open in terminal”.
settings.mergeToolExecutablePathPlaceholder: Leave empty if on PATH; otherwise full path to BComp.exe or WinMergeU.exe
settings.mergeToolExecutablePathHint: Mergetool in Changes runs git mergetool with this preset. If Git cannot find the tool, set the path here.
settings.performanceLead: Max commits loaded for “All commits”. Lower for huge repos; use “Load more” in that view.
settings.dataLead: Workspace state (open tabs, filters) is stored in localStorage on this machine.
settings.dataMuted: This does not remove saved theme/diff defaults or change files on disk.
